titleStmt.title: Letter from Robert Brown to Robert Chalmers, 26 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Robert Brown

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Robert Brown, the assistant manager at the National Margarine Company Ltd., to Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938). In the letter Brown attempts to acquire compensation for a attending the trail of Henry McCambley (b.1871). Brown was required to attend the hearing as a witness in Belfast. The letter discusses the money Brown lost stating that despite what he is being told, he has yet to receive the full compensation and requests the remittance to be paid.Sir Robert Chalmers was sent to Ireland as replacement under-secretary for Sir Matthew Nathan, who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary, following the outbreak of the Easter Rising in 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5613/9069

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Ack. and state that
T. E has nothing to
add to the replies sent
to his previous letters on
the subject.
I St
29 - 5 - '16 THE NATIONAL MARGARINE COMPANY LIMITED 10 QUALITY STREET LEITH 26th May 1916 Dear Sir. I duly received your favour of 23rd. inst. I am glad
that it is now clear to you that I received only Â£2/13/4 in
respect of travelling and subsistence expences. I gave you this
information in a perfectly plain manner in my letter of 15th.
April and repeated the statement to you per my letter of 22nd.
April I shall be glad if you will now give instructions for the
sum of Â£1/1/0 to be remitted to me, with respect to loss of time
and loss of earnings when serving the police, being absent from
home from 8th. Oct. to 10th. Oct. performing the journey to Belfast
and back in accordance with the desire of the Police Authorities. I was informed before I set out on the journey that my
expenses would be paid, and only, viz:- Â£2/13/4 has been paid. I wish payment of the difference, and trust you will now give
the necessary instructions to save further trouble. Yours truly, Rot. Brown
